scope.release_candidate_intent: Define the initial FreeCAD/Python support promise and the bounded B14/B15 ingress window before modular source or persistence migration starts.
scope.current_effect: Development evidence and future implementation gates only. This contract does not alter B14, B15, any existing FCStd, configuration, Addon package or current macro behaviour.
scope.support_meaning: A release-supported workflow must pass both a qualified-runtime gate and a supported-document/schema gate before the Workbench may mutate, migrate or export.
scope.licensing_separation: Compatibility support does not grant project-cleared output status and does not change any provenance, dependency-manifest or rights gate.

fields.freecadmin: Required earliest FreeCAD release with which the Addon works.
fields.freecadmax: Optional latest FreeCAD release with which the Addon works.
fields.pythonmin: Required earliest Python version needed by the Addon.

initial_package_manifest_intent.phase_10_rule: Generate the real package.xml only in the Workbench packaging phase and revalidate these values against then-current official schema and qualification evidence.

runtime_baseline.runtime_policy: The Workbench uses the Python, Qt, OpenCASCADE and Coin stack bundled by the supported FreeCAD distribution; users are not asked to install a separate runtime Python.

standalone_development_floor.scope: Repository tools, AST/analytical tests and domain-package import checks only; this does not by itself qualify a FreeCAD host.

qualified_profiles.host_distribution_evidence: Linux Mint 22.3 x86_64 host running the stable org.freecad.FreeCAD Flatpak
qualification_basis: Phase 0 macro syntax, B15 structural/analytical and FreeCADCmd smoke checks
B14 isolated real-GUI workflow and performance recipes
B14-to-B15 real-GUI, reuse and save/reopen acceptance
Phase 1 B14 copied-document plain-line, straight/station and turnout oracles

runtime_baseline.support_rule: Only an exact qualified profile is initially release-supported. A newer FreeCAD patch, another bundled Python/Qt/OCC/Coin stack, another package channel, OS or architecture is unqualified until its required matrix passes and this contract is deliberately revised.
runtime_baseline.unqualified_runtime_rule: Report every observed mismatch, preserve the document untouched and disable Track Template mutation, migration and production export; do not guess compatibility from a higher version number.

legacy_document_window.mixed_b14_b15_rule: A B15 action may retain inherited B14 objects and add or retag only changed B15 objects. The exact mixed set is therefore an intended B15 ingress form, not automatically a conflict.

version_detection.scope: Read-only preflight over objects whose GeneratedBy equals the declared generator_id and over recognised JSON payloads on those objects.
version_detection.generator_version_rule: GeneratorVersion stores a descriptive string; its first whitespace-delimited token is the macro version token.
version_detection.json_version_rule: Recognised macro_version and source_macro_version fields carry version tokens and must not contradict object evidence.
version_detection.schema_rule: Every encountered required payload must have a recognised schema and internally consistent stable identities; derived payloads may be discarded only when canonical inputs are sufficient to regenerate them.
version_detection.foreign_object_rule: FreeCAD objects not owned by the declared generator_id do not classify the document and must remain untouched.

classification.condition: All owned version evidence is one accepted version set, every required schema is recognised and the entity family has a passing migration fixture.
classification.write_policy: Migrate only a user-approved copy/new document through the future versioned migrator; never rewrite the source in place.

classification.condition: The document is pre-B14, versionless, contains an unknown/future token, lacks sufficient canonical inputs, or contains a family without an accepted migration fixture.
classification.write_policy: Allow a compatibility report and ordinary FreeCAD inspection only; no Track Template mutation, migration or production export.

classification.condition: Owned version/schema evidence conflicts, is malformed or is corrupt.
classification.write_policy: Stop before a transaction, preserve the document and report exact objects/properties/findings.

migration_invariants: Open and inspect the source read-only before starting any transaction.
Require an explicit user-selected copy/new target and retain the original byte-for-byte.
Validate the complete target model before one atomic commit; abort and remove the target on failure.
Preserve stable railway identities, ordering, units, coordinate frames, topology intent, production selections and user-authored metadata.
Treat legacy Part shapes as comparison/recovery evidence, not canonical model state; regenerate derived views and exact geometry only when sufficient canonical inputs exist.
Do not silently drop an unsupported entity, property, payload, finding or foreign FreeCAD object.
Save/reopen, undo/redo, failure recovery and legacy-versus-canonical semantic comparison must pass for each supported entity family before RC support is claimed.
legacy_document_window.family_evidence_rule: B14/B15 identify the outer ingress window, not blanket migration coverage. Phase 4 must attach deterministic migration fixtures for every advertised entity family; a document containing any unqualified family remains inspection-only.
legacy_document_window.successor_target_status: The canonical successor document schema and migration implementation belong to Phase 4; B16 is a development composition checkpoint, not another legacy source generation.

external_configuration_window.scope_rule: Configuration, last-input and preset support is content/schema based and is deliberately separate from FCStd document support.

configuration_json.input: No configuration root but required transition/radius/angle/main_width fields
Future, negative, missing, non-integer, unsafe or malformed schema/data

named_preset_library.input: Other, missing or malformed library schema/root

enforcement_and_requalification.phase_2: Use this baseline for standalone record/type choices and a development-only runtime guard; do not broaden the matrix by assumption.
enforcement_and_requalification.phase_4: Implement the document detector, versioned canonical schema, B14/B15 family migrations, copied-target transaction and migration fixtures.
enforcement_and_requalification.phase_10: Generate and validate package.xml, requalify the supported platform matrix, publish upgrade/recovery guidance and bind unsupported-host diagnostics into the Workbench.
requalification_triggers: Any FreeCAD version change, including a patch release
Any bundled Python, Qt/PySide, OpenCASCADE or Coin version change
Another OS, architecture, package channel or FreeCAD distribution
A new runtime dependency or a change to the minimum Python feature set
Any persisted schema, migration rule, entity family or compatibility-launcher change
required_matrix: Standalone domain/API import and analytical tests
FreeCADCmd structural, document and save/reopen tests
Validate/Export equivalence, transactional failure and cleanup
B14 and B15 copied-document migrations for every advertised entity family
Unsupported, future, conflicting, corrupt and exact change-back cases

known_evidence_gaps: No Windows, macOS, non-Flatpak Linux or other architecture is qualified yet.
No FreeCAD release other than 1.1.1 is qualified yet.
No pre-B14 or versionless FCStd is promised as a migration source.
The B14/B15 successor document detector and migrator do not exist yet.
B15 save/reopen evidence is bounded to the accepted crossover chair delta; family-by-family migration fixtures remain Phase 4 work.
